questions.question: Which sentence is closest to a prayer?
questions.correct_answer: 3. May my friend get well soon.
questions.explanation: This sentence shows a desire for someone's well-being, which is the essence of a prayer.

questions.question: What does the poet pray for in her poem?
questions.correct_answer: Friendship and making friends' wishes come true
questions.explanation: The poet prays that friendship should always be an important part of her life and that she can make her friends' wishes come true.

questions.question: What does the poet want to do for her friends?
questions.explanation: The poet wants to help her friends achieve their goals and desires.

questions.question: How does the poet feel about special friends?
questions.explanation: The poet feels that having special friends is a blessing.

questions.question: What does the poet use to realise what friends can be?
questions.explanation: The poet wants to use her heart and intuition to understand true friendship.

questions.question: What does the speaker do when she realises what friends can be?
questions.explanation: The speaker accepts and loves her friends for who they are, without judgment.

questions.question: How will the speaker make her friends' wishes come true?
questions.explanation: The speaker wants to put in effort to help her friends achieve their goals.

questions.question: What does the poet want her heart to do?
questions.correct_answer: To realise what friends can be
questions.explanation: The poet wants her heart to understand true friendship and accept it without judgment.

questions.question: How does the speaker feel about sharing hopes and plans with friends?
questions.correct_answer: Wants to do more than just share
questions.explanation: The speaker wants to actively support and help her friends achieve their goals.

questions.question: What will my friends feel if their wishes come true?
questions.explanation: When the speaker's friends' wishes are fulfilled, they will likely feel happy and satisfied.

notes.title: Key Concept 1
notes.content: A prayer can be a desire for someone's well-being or happiness.

notes.title: Key Concept 2
notes.content: The poet prays that friendship should always be an important part of her life.

notes.title: Key Concept 3
notes.content: The speaker wants to use her heart and intuition to understand true friendship.

notes.title: Key Concept 4
notes.content: The poet feels that having special friends is a blessing.

notes.title: Key Concept 5
notes.content: The speaker accepts and loves her friends for who they are, without judgment.

notes.title: Key Concept 6
notes.content: The poet wants to actively support and help her friends achieve their goals.

notes.title: Key Concept 7
notes.content: True friendship involves accepting and loving others for who they are.

notes.title: Key Concept 8
notes.content: Friendship should always be an important part of one's life.

notes.title: Key Concept 9
notes.content: The poet wants to use her heart and mind to understand true friendship.

notes.title: Key Concept 10
notes.content: Acceptance and love are key components of true friendship.

facts.fact: A prayer can be a desire for someone's well-being or happiness.
The poet prays that friendship should always be an important part of her life.
The speaker wants to use her heart and intuition to understand true friendship.
Having special friends is a blessing, according to the poet.
True friendship involves accepting and loving others for who they are.
Friendship should always be an important part of one's life.
The poet wants to actively support and help her friends achieve their goals.
Acceptance and love are key components of true friendship.
The speaker feels happy when her friends' wishes come true.
The poet's poem is a speech on the importance of friendship.
